raw_responses.title: Coanda effect (Wikipedia)
raw_responses.snippet: Source-domain: jet entrainment, pressure asymmetry, surface attachment.

raw_responses.title: Fundamentals in Coanda Flap Design
raw_responses.snippet: Source-domain: keep flow attached over highly deflected flap by blowing jet tangentially over surface contour.

raw_responses.title: Design and Experimental Characterization of an Actuation System for Internally Blown Coanda Flap
raw_responses.snippet: Actuator system for 3D flow control of blown Coanda flap; lift increase ΔCa = 0.57.

raw_responses.title: When Attention Sink Emerges (ICLR 2025)
raw_responses.snippet: Attention sink: strong attention to initial tokens regardless of semantic relevance; emerges during pretraining.

raw_responses.title: Structural Origin of Attention Sink: Variance Discrepancy, Super Neurons, Dimension Disparity
raw_responses.snippet: Sink anchors at position zero in causal decoders; causal chain from variance discrepancy to QK locking traces origin to structural asymmetry of causal masking.

raw_responses.title: Attention Sinks and Compression Valleys in LLMs are Two Sides of the Same Coin
raw_responses.snippet: Three-phase processing: broad mixing (early), compressed computation (middle), selective refinement (late); sinks ↔ compression valleys via massive activations in residual stream.

raw_responses.title: ROLL Attention Illuminates LLM Reasoning: Preplan-and-Anchor
raw_responses.snippet: Attention spikes at semantic boundaries; anchor tokens organize downstream inference.

raw_responses.title: Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks
raw_responses.snippet: StreamingLLM allows finite-window LLMs to generalize to infinite sequence lengths via attention sink retention.
